title: Binary Place Values
description: Understanding 8-bit binary place values and how bits build denary numbers.

steps.title: Binary Places
steps.content:
Binary place values

Binary uses only two digits: 0 and 1. Each column is worth twice as much as the column to its right.

1286432168421
10101001

A 1 means include that place value. A 0 means ignore it.

steps.title: Step Through: Read Bits
steps.content:
Step through an example

Click through each stage to see how the total is built.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9pv-stepper-1',steps:[{title:'Start with the place values',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,0,1,0,0,1]),note:'The headers show what each column is worth.'},{title:'Keep only the 1 columns',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,0,1,0,0,1]),note:'The active columns are 128, 32, 8 and 1.'},{title:'Add the active columns',html:'

128 + 32 + 8 + 1 = 169

',note:'So 10101001 has the denary value 169.'}]});

steps.title: Step Through: Missing Places
steps.content:
Another step-through question

This time focus on identifying active place values before adding.
steps.js:
BinaryLesson.createStepper({containerId:'y9pv-stepper-2',steps:[{title:'Read the bits',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,1,1,0,1,0]),note:'Look for the columns with a 1.'},{title:'Name the active columns',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,1,1,0,1,0]),note:'64, 16, 8 and 2 are active.'},{title:'Add them',html:'

64 + 16 + 8 + 2 = 90

',note:'So 01011010 is worth 90.'}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 1
steps.content:
Checked practice: active places

Type the denary total for each binary number. You will get feedback after each answer.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y9pv-practice-a',questions:[{prompt:'What is the denary value?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,1,0,1,0,0,0,0]),answer:'80',right:'Correct: 64 + 16 = 80.'},{prompt:'What is the denary value?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,0,0,0,1,0,1]),answer:'133',right:'Correct: 128 + 4 + 1 = 133.'},{prompt:'What is the denary value?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,1,0,1,0,1,0]),answer:'42',right:'Correct: 32 + 8 + 2 = 42.'}]});

steps.title: Checked Practice 2
steps.content:
Checked practice: build numbers

Type the 8-bit binary number that matches the active place values.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y9pv-practice-b',questions:[{prompt:'Use 128, 16, 4 and 1.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0]),answer:'10010101',right:'Correct: those columns are switched on.'},{prompt:'Use 64, 32 and 8.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0]),answer:'01101000',right:'Correct: 64 + 32 + 8.'},{prompt:'Use 16, 8, 4, 2 and 1.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0]),answer:'00011111',right:'Correct: the final five columns are on.'}]});

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 generated questions correctly. The step will mark itself complete automatically when you hit the target.

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer all questions. Feedback appears after each one.
steps.js: BinaryLesson.createPractice({containerId:'y9pv-exam',questions:[{prompt:'Convert to denary.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,1,0,0,1,0,0,0]),answer:'200',right:'128 + 64 + 8 = 200.'},{prompt:'Convert to denary.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,1,0,1,0,1]),answer:'21',right:'16 + 4 + 1 = 21.'},{prompt:'Build 128, 16, 4 and 1 as binary.',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([0,0,0,0,0,0,0,0]),answer:'10010101',right:'Correct.'},{prompt:'Which denary value is shown?',html:BinaryLesson.bitTable([1,0,1,0,0,0,0,1]),answer:'161',right:'128 + 32 + 1 = 161.'}]});

quizQuestions.q: Which place value is furthest left in an 8-bit binary number?
In 01010000, which place values are active?
What does a 0 mean in a binary column?
options: Do not include that place value
